steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes a noun phrase about specific fertilizers. The modifier must be restrictive (specifying which fertilizers) rather than nonrestrictive (adding extra detail about already-identified ones), because the study focuses on fertilizers that contain particular macronutrients.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "fertilizers. Containing": placing ‘Containing macronutrients’ after a period creates a fragment with no main clause. ✗
  • "fertilizers, containing": the comma makes the modifier nonrestrictive, implying all fertilizers are already identified — too broad. ✗
  • "fertilizers containing": no comma, so the participial phrase is restrictive and specifies exactly which fertilizers the study examines. ✓
  • "fertilizers, which contain": like B, the comma creates a nonrestrictive modifier that loses the necessary specificity. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
